title: angelica adzharica
intro: Angelica adzharica, also known as Caucasian angelica, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Russia and Georgia, due to its rich store of medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Angelica adzharica include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe digestive problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are typically dried and used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams and ointments.
